items.title: Niranjan Jyoti Appointed NCBC Chairperson, Kiran Umesh Mahalle Becomes Member
items.whyInNews: In 2026, Niranjan Jyoti assumed charge as Chairperson and Kiran Umesh Mahalle as Member of the National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC), a constitutional body under Article 338B.
items.summary: The National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC), granted constitutional status by the 102nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 2018, is responsible for safeguarding the interests of Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBCs). The commission has five members appointed by the President and plays a key role in policy consultation, grievance redressal, and socio-economic development of backward classes.
keyPoints: Niranjan Jyoti appointed Chairperson and Kiran Umesh Mahalle appointed Member of NCBC in 2026
NCBC is a constitutional body under Article 338B after 102nd Amendment Act, 2018
Commission consists of 5 members appointed by the President with a tenure of 3 years
NCBC has powers of a civil court including summoning witnesses and examining documents
importantPoints: 102nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 2018 gave constitutional status to NCBC
Article 338B provides constitutional basis for NCBC
Article 342A empowers President to specify SEBCs for states and UTs
105th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2021 restored states’ power to identify OBCs
NCBC ensures welfare, protection, and grievance redressal for backward classes in India
items.examRelevance: Important for SSC Polity covering constitutional bodies, Articles 338, 338A, 338B, 342A, and amendments related to OBCs and backward class welfare
